Biblical Prophecy · Hermeneutical Comparison · Scholarly Analysis

Evangelical Futurism (Dispensationalism)— versus —SDA Historicism

A comparative analysis of prophetic timelines and their biblical foundations
Evangelical / Dispensationalist

Futurist Prophetic Timeline

Origins: J.N. Darby (1830s), C.I. Scofield, Hal Lindsey, Tim LaHaye
Loading timeline...
SDA / Protestant Historicism

SDA Historicist Timeline

Origins: Reformation (Luther, Calvin, Newton), Miller Movement, SDA pioneers (1844–present)
Loading timeline...

⚑ The Central Hermeneutical Divide

Futurism requires a 2,000+ year gap between Daniel's 69th and 70th week — an insertion with zero textual basis. Historicism reads all 70 weeks as contiguous and finds every event historically verified in the 1st century AD. The entire futurist system depends on this unproven gap.

Plain Prophecy · Biblical Prophecy ResourceDan 8:14 · Dan 9:24–27 · Rev 14:6–12 · Rev 20 · Heb 8–9plainprophecy.com · British English
